dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Onix eyes are drawn on segments[0] (the base/tail) instead of segments[segments.length-1] (the head/top). The task specifies Onix as a serpentine chain from bottom to top, so eyes should be on the topmost (head) segment. Largest Onix circle radius is ~10px, not the specified ~22px; though on an 80x80 canvas, ~22px radius may not fit cleanly with 7 stacked segments.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: The generate-gym-sprites.mjs script has no try/catch around file I/O. A permission error or disk-full condition would produce an unhandled rejection rather than a clean error message. Acceptable for a dev-only build script but could be improved.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 6 sprites render at runtime replacing colored rectangles. The Onix sprite shows eyes on the tail segment (bottom) instead of the head (top), which looks wrong to anyone familiar with Onix. Still recognizably a pixel-art chain shape rather than a rectangle.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: In drawOnixFront(), the comment '// Eyes on the top (head) segment' refers to segments[0], which is actually the base/bottom segment. The code variable naming and the segment array ordering are inconsistent: segments[0] is labelled '// base (largest)' at y=72, but the eye placement comment calls it the 'top (head)'. The loop comment 'Draw segments from bottom to top (bottom-most first so upper overlap)' is also reversed — the loop iterates segments.length-1 down to 0, drawing tip-first and base last, which is fine visually but the comment is wrong.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: AssetLoader tests were correctly updated to reflect 24 IDs and the new paths for 74, 75, 95. All 289 tests pass. A test that actually checks the sprite files exist on disk (e.g., fs.existsSync) would add an extra layer of protection against accidentally deleting generated files.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No security concerns. No user input, no API keys, no network calls, purely static asset generation.

issues.description: Onix eyes are drawn on segments[0] (y≈72, the base/tail) instead of the head. The comment reads '// Eyes on the top (head) segment' but segments[0] is explicitly labelled '// base (largest)' at the bottom of the canvas. The horn is correctly placed on segments[segments.length-1] (the tip/top), creating an inconsistency: eyes on the tail, horn on the head.
issues.fix_guidance: Change `const [hx, hy] = [segments[0][0], segments[0][1]]` to `const headSeg = segments[segments.length - 1]; const [hx, hy] = [headSeg[0], headSeg[1]]` so eyes and horn are both on the topmost segment (the head). Then remove the redundant `const headSeg` line below.

issues.description: The drawOnixFront loop comment 'Draw segments from bottom to top (bottom-most first so upper overlap)' is incorrect. The loop runs `for (let i = segments.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)` which iterates from the tip (index 6) down to the base (index 0), meaning the base is drawn last — not 'bottom-most first'.
issues.fix_guidance: Correct the comment to: '// Draw tip first so the larger base is painted on top (overlap order)'. No code change needed — the drawing order is actually correct; only the comment is wrong.

summary: All functional requirements are met: 6 PNG sprites (all > 500 bytes) exist for Geodude (#74), Graveler (#75), and Onix (#95) in both front and back views; AssetManifest.pokemonIds includes 74, 75, 95; generate-assets.mjs includes the new entries; package.json has the convenience script; and all 289 existing tests continue to pass. The two P2 issues are cosmetic: Onix's eyes appear on the tail segment instead of the head due to a variable naming confusion, and a loop comment is factually reversed. Neither affects game stability, performance, or the core requirement of rendering a distinct sprite instead of a colored rectangle.
